title: Logging Authentication Attempts and Transaction State Changes
description: The system shall record all authentication attempts (successful/failed) and all transaction state changes with timestamps.
rationale: To audit system activity and ensure security compliance.
business_value: Improves the ability to monitor and review user actions for security purposes, aiding in compliance with security standards.

source.description: As a system administrator I need to record all authentication attempts (successful/failed) and all transaction state changes with timestamps So that I can audit system activity and ensure security compliance

acceptance_criteria: Given a user attempts to authenticate, When the authentication attempt completes (success or failure), Then the system records the attempt with a timestamp and outcome
Given a transaction changes state, When the state transition occurs, Then the system records the new state with a timestamp
postconditions: All authentication attempts are recorded in the logs
All transaction state changes are recorded in the logs
Each log entry includes a precise timestamp
Logs are stored in a tamper-proof or append-only format
functional_constraints: The system must record both successful and failed login attempts
The system must capture all transaction state changes (e.g., pending, completed, failed)
security_requirements: Logs should be stored in a tamper-proof or append-only format
edge_cases: What happens if the system is under heavy load and cannot keep up with logging requests?
How should the system handle network failures during logging operations?
What if there are concurrent login attempts or state changes happening at the same time?

interfaces.name: Audit Log Database
interfaces.description: The system must be able to write and read from this database to store authentication attempts and transaction state changes.

interfaces.name: Authentication API
interfaces.description: The system must call into this API to determine the outcome of user authentication attempts.

test_cases.description: Verify that successful login attempts are recorded with timestamps.
test_cases.expected_result: A log entry is created in the Audit Log Database for each successful login attempt, including a precise timestamp and 'success' outcome.

test_cases.description: Verify that failed login attempts are recorded with timestamps.
test_cases.expected_result: A log entry is created in the Audit Log Database for each failed login attempt, including a precise timestamp and 'failure' outcome.

test_cases.description: Verify that transaction state changes are recorded with timestamps.
test_cases.expected_result: A log entry is created in the Audit Log Database for each transaction state change, including a precise timestamp and the new state.
